title: hedyotis fruticulosa var. yapensis
intro: Hedyotis fruticulosa var. yapensis, also known as Yap hedyotis, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the island of Guam and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, specifically on the island of Rota. It belongs to the family Rubiaceae and is a type of flowering plant. Yap hedyotis has been traditionally used in Chamorro medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, sore throat, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, highlighting its potential as a natural remedy for various health benefits.
